meta.title: Mikita Lashytski — websites, stores and automation for small business
meta.description: I build landing pages, business sites, Shopify stores, bots and practical automations. Start with free guides or send a project for review.
meta.ogTagline: Websites · Shopify · Bots · Automation · SEO · AI search

hero.subtitle: I turn small-business ideas into live pages, Shopify stores, bots and useful automations: offer, design, code, domain, forms, payments, SEO and launch.

manifesto.title: A site is the surface. The value is underneath.
manifesto.body: Anyone can buy a template. The work that actually moves money is invisible: clean operations, technical SEO, machine-readable data, and being the answer an AI gives — not the link buried on page two.

points.title: Operations, not just pixels
points.text: Sourcing, EAN/GTIN, product cards, marketplace listings, payments and delivery — I handle the shop work behind the front-end.

points.title: Search visibility
points.text: Technical SEO, indexing, Core Web Vitals and rankings that you can read in Search Console, not just promise.

points.title: AI visibility (AEO/GEO)
points.text: Structured for ChatGPT, Perplexity and AI Overviews — llms.txt, clean schema, and content that gets cited.

points.title: Structured data
points.text: Schema.org on everything: Product, Offer, Review, Course, Event, FAQ — eligible for rich results across the catalog.

projects.title: Four systems. Four markets.
projects.subtitle: E-commerce, B2B, booking and payments — each one with the internals to match.

metrics.title: Numbers I can show you the source of
metrics.subtitle: Every metric is tagged with where it comes from — marketplace, operations, Lighthouse or Search Console.

underHood.title: What you don't see on the cover
underHood.subtitle: This very site is the demo: perfect Lighthouse, JSON-LD on itself, hreflang across locales, sitemap and llms.txt.

cards.title: Structured data on itself
cards.text: This page ships Organization + Person + WebSite JSON-LD. View source and you'll find it.

cards.title: Built for AI answers
cards.text: A public llms.txt and clean robots rules so GPTBot and PerplexityBot can read and cite the work.

cards.title: Multilingual by architecture
cards.text: EN / PL / RU / UA with full hreflang — and the routing scales to every EU language by adding one file.

cards.title: Performance as a feature
cards.text: Static rendering, no bloat, Core Web Vitals in the green. Speed is a ranking signal and a UX signal.

cta.title: Got a project that needs the whole stack?
cta.subtitle: Not just a designer, not just a coder — someone who owns the result end to end.

projectPage.schemaNote: Schema.org types implemented with rich-result eligibility.
projectPage.eventBotNote: Gemini bot for automated event publishing — a module inside the same infrastructure.
projectPage.metricsNote: Source tags show where each number comes from. Yellow badge = estimated, not verified.
projectPage.salesNoteBody: Send the current site, idea or blocker. I will tell you whether DIY, audit/rescue or a full build makes more sense.

mnsk7-tools.tagline: CNC milling-cutter shop with fast Polish delivery, China sourcing, EAN/GTIN data and Allegro listings behind it.
mnsk7-tools.summary: More than a website: the project included sourcing cutters from China, assigning EAN/GTIN codes, building product cards and listing across both the shop and Allegro — backed by a trust block of real marketplace reviews.

before: Marketplace-only or scattered listings without a unified catalog
Manual China sourcing without EAN/GTIN standardization
No loyalty program or SEO category architecture on the site
after: mnsk7-tools.pl + Allegro with 3500+ orders and 383 reviews
425 SKUs with EAN/GTIN and unified sourcing ops
Categories by material/application, 5/10/15% loyalty, Super Sprzedawca

role: Built the storefront with categories organised by material, application and tool type.
Created the product cards and the loyalty/discount program for repeat buyers.
Engineered the trust layer using real Allegro reviews and seller status.
Set up fast-shipping and B2B purchasing flows.
ops: Sourced milling cutters directly from China.
Assigned EAN/GTIN codes to every product.
Built and standardised 400+ product cards.
Listed across the shop and Allegro as a Super Sprzedawca.
seo: Category architecture built around buyer intent (material + application).
Product schema with ratings and reviews for rich results.
Fast pages and clean indexing for high-intent CNC queries.
ai: Product / Offer / Review schema feeds AI shopping answers.
Specs (diameter, shank, material) structured for precise machine reading.
mnsk7-tools.problem: Needed a CNC tool shop that covered China sourcing, EAN/GTIN, product cards, Allegro listings and the website — not only a storefront.
mnsk7-tools.whatIDid: Built the site, sourcing ops, 425 SKUs with EAN, Allegro integration, loyalty program and category SEO structure.
mnsk7-tools.result: 3500+ Allegro orders, 383 reviews, live shop + marketplace, and a repeatable sourcing/listing process.
mnsk7-tools.nextSteps: Public analytics screenshots, site GSC metrics, automated reporting.
mnsk7-tools.cardDesc: CNC shop + Allegro ops: 3500+ orders, 425 SKUs, EAN/GTIN sourcing process.

popular.tagline: Sellable proof: two-domain sales system with own box office and Gemini publishing bot — AI organic converted to ticket sales in 2 weeks.
popular.summary: Built for a theatre studio selling to emigrants: course vitrine + proprietary ticketing, admin, check-in, PL/UA/RU i18n. Gemini bot automates ~4 events/week (photo+text → pages → Bing ping). Result: AI search traffic → ticket sales within 2 weeks of launch.

before: Instagram-only presence — no owned website or search footprint
Commission ticketing platforms that take a cut and bring no niche emigrant traffic
No course vitrine, no owned sales funnel, no on-site ticket verification
~4 events/week entered manually — slow, repetitive CMS work every week
after: Two sites: popularpoet.pl (course vitrine) + populartickets.pl (own box office)
Supabase backend, admin panel, Resend notifications and check-in at the door
Gemini event bot: photo + text dump → structured events → live pages → Bing ping
Google Business Profile, trilingual PL/UA/RU, AI organic traffic → ticket sales in 2 weeks

systemFlow.title: Digital infrastructure flow
steps: Instagram-only presence / course demand from emigrant audience
popularpoet.pl — course vitrine and enrollment funnel
~4 events/week — manual entry was slow; need automation for weekly show publishing
Photo + text dump → Gemini (free tier) parses structured events
Ready event pages published → Bing IndexNow / search-engine ping
Ticket need → populartickets.pl (own box office, no commission)
Google / AI visibility → organic traffic → ticket sales

popular.problem: Popular Poet is a theatre studio for Ukrainian/Russian-speaking emigrants in Poland. Their entire internet presence was Instagram — no owned site, no search indexing, no sales infrastructure. Commission ticketing platforms charge fees and don't drive traffic to a niche emigrant theatre audience. They needed a course vitrine, their own ticket office, and visibility in Google and AI search — not another social-only funnel. On top of that, ~4 events per week had to be created manually — tedious data entry that ate hours every week.
popular.whatIDid: Built popularpoet.pl as a trilingual course showcase (PL/UA/RU) and populartickets.pl as a proprietary box office on Next.js + Supabase + Vercel. Implemented admin panel for events and courses, Resend for transactional email, on-site check-in for ticket verification, and structured data for local + multilingual SEO. Set up Google Business Profile. To kill weekly publishing friction, built a Gemini-powered event bot on the free tier: drop photos and raw text, the bot parses structured events, outputs ready pages, and pings Bing (and similar indexing) about new URLs. The system replaces commission platforms with owned infrastructure that can be found, cited, and kept fresh by AI search — without manual event entry.
popular.result: Two live domains — popularpoet.pl and populartickets.pl — with full operational stack: admin, check-in, Resend, trilingual content and GBP. Event publishing automated for ~4 shows/week: photo/text → Gemini → pages → Bing ping — hours saved on weekly ops. Real outcome: within 2 weeks of launch, organic traffic from AI search converted to ticket sales. Not a brochure site — a digital infrastructure layer for emigrant theatre with automation-first weekly publishing.

Google Business Profile wired into local search footprint.

Owned infrastructure replaces commission platforms that AI can't recommend as primary source.

alesyatakun.tagline: A psychologist's site with secure online payment and a pay-what-fits booking flow.
alesyatakun.summary: A focused personal-brand site for a gestalt therapist: clear service descriptions, individual and group formats, and secure online payment via Alfa-Bank (3-D Secure) with a flexible 'free payment' amount.

before: Booking and payment via DMs — no structure
No trust-first site with FAQ and schema.org
No secure online payment with flexible amount
after: 3-D Secure payment + flexible 'free payment' in BYN
Telegram booking + FAQ/schema for search and AI

role: Built the personal-brand site with a calm, trust-first design.
Implemented secure online payment with a pay-what-fits amount.
Structured services, pricing and the booking process.
Wrote FAQ and process content for clarity and SEO.
ops: Secure payments via Alfa-Bank (no card data stored).
Individual sessions and group formats with clear pricing.
Booking funnel through Telegram + email confirmation.
seo: Person + Service schema for entity recognition.
FAQ schema for high-intent therapy queries.
Local relevance for Minsk + online clients.
ai: Person / MedicalBusiness / Service schema for AI entity answers.
FAQ content structured to be quoted directly by assistants.
alesyatakun.problem: The specialist needed a trust-first site with online payment and booking — without complex CRM and with clear UX.
alesyatakun.whatIDid: Built personal-brand site, 3-D Secure payments, FAQ, schema.org and Telegram booking funnel.
alesyatakun.result: Live site with payments, two service formats and structure for search and AI visibility.
alesyatakun.nextSteps: Mobile screenshots, GSC, content expansion for local queries.
alesyatakun.cardDesc: Psychologist site with 3-D Secure payments and flexible booking flow.

event-bot.tagline: Gemini bot for Popular Poet: photo + text → structured events → live pages → Bing ping.
event-bot.summary: Event automation layer inside the Popular Poet case (~4 shows/week). Instead of manual CMS entry, the team drops photos and raw text into a bot on free Gemini — it parses structured events, publishes ready pages on popularpoet.pl / populartickets.pl, and pings Bing for indexing. See the full case: /projects/popular.
event-bot.problem: Popular Poet publishes ~4 events per week. Each one was entered manually in the CMS — slow, repetitive work that ate hours every week.
event-bot.whatIDid: Built a Gemini-powered event bot (free tier) for Popular Poet: photo + text dump → parsed event fields → CMS publish → Bing IndexNow / search ping. Integrated into the existing Next.js + Supabase stack.
event-bot.result: Weekly show publishing automated — hours saved on ops. Fresh event pages go live faster and get indexed sooner. Part of the Popular Poet digital infrastructure, not a standalone toy project.
event-bot.nextSteps: Screenshots of bot input/output, time-saved metrics, link back to full Popular Poet case study.
role: Designed photo + text → Gemini → structured event pipeline for Popular Poet.
Integrated bot output with Next.js CMS and publish flow.
Wired Bing IndexNow / search-engine ping after each new event page.
ops: ~4 events/week without manual CMS entry.
Photo + text dump as the only ops input.
Automated publish to popularpoet.pl / populartickets.pl.
seo: New event pages enter site structure automatically.
Event schema + Bing ping for faster indexing of weekly listings.
ai: Gemini parses unstructured photo + text into machine-readable events.
Structured events feed AI search citations on Popular Poet domains.
event-bot.cardDesc: Photo + text → Gemini → live event pages → Bing ping. ~4 shows/week automated.

kayer-pl.tagline: Polish market migration: WooCommerce → Shopify, BaseLinker sync, AI content automation for professional nail cosmetics.
kayer-pl.summary: KAYER.PL: migrated from WooCommerce/WordPress to Shopify with BaseLinker catalog sync, automation-first content pipeline (Python + OpenAI) for PL/EN, B2B salon verification and faster promotion rollout.

before: WooCommerce / WordPress legacy store on KAYER.PL
Manual editing of products, descriptions and promotions
Heavy update process — no unified pipeline across PL / UA / EN
after: AI-assisted content workflow: Python scripts + OpenAI API
Faster promotions, agentic ops, UA market with Flu.io / Dilovod / Nova Poshta

about.title: Five steps from idea to deployment
about.intro: I build working digital systems for small business — landings, stores, bots and automations. Here is the process before we start.

steps.title: You send the idea
steps.text: Raw text is fine: landing, store, bot, migration or audit.

steps.title: I structure it
steps.text: Pages, features, data, integrations, timeline and tech plan.

steps.title: We validate fast
steps.text: Prototype or audit draft — shape before a 40-slide deck.

steps.title: Build and deploy
steps.text: Site, forms, payments, bots, SEO base, domain, instructions.

steps.title: Iterate for results
steps.text: Metrics, content, automation, SEO/GEO — what brings leads and sales.

values: One person who owns operations, code and growth.
Honest metrics with the source labelled — no vanity numbers.
Sites that are fast, structured and ready for the AI era.
Multilingual architecture that scales to new markets.

contact.title: Show me the project — I will map the next steps
contact.subtitle: Send the idea, deadline, budget and current links. I will answer with the cleanest path: landing, store, bot, SEO fix or full rebuild.
contact.note: Contact details are placeholders for now — replace them in src/content/site.ts.

confusion.title: Report unclear text
confusion.success: Thanks — we'll clarify and update the guide
confusion.error: Could not send. Try again later.
confusion.privacy: Only the selected fragment is sent

footer.built: Built with Next.js — JSON-LD, hreflang and llms.txt included.
